acquisition method: Data collection was run on TOP 20 Data‐Dependent Acquisition (DDA) mode.
alkylation reagent: the inability of the rescoring platforms—especially Oktoberfest and inSPIRE—to process modifications other than fixed carbamidomethylation (C) and oxidation (M).
cleavage agent: Target proteins were digested (trypsin), shuffled, and reassembled.
collision energy: further fragmented with higher energy collisional dissociation (HCD) at 27 normalized collision energy (NCE).
fragmentation method: further fragmented with higher energy collisional dissociation (HCD)
instrument: data acquisition was done by a Thermo Scientific Q Exactive Plus Orbitrap Mass Spectrometer.
ionization type: hooked up to a Nanospray Flex ion source.
labeling: the results clearly indicated an important increase (40%–53%) in the number of peptide identifications when using a data‐driven rescoring.
